page: Amazon Web Services x8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong processing capabilities, particularly in single-core and memory-bound operations. The instance achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing tests. Multi-threaded performance is also robust, with strong ratings in bzip3 decompression and Passmark CPU benchmarks. For machine learning workloads, the processor's AVX-512 and AMX extensions support strong prompt processing and text generation speeds for small to medium language models, though performance drops to the bottom quartile for large-scale 70B model text generation due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x8i.8xlarge offers a dense memory profile relative to its compute allocation, making it highly suitable for memory-heavy workloads without the overhead of excess vCPUs. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, requiring network-attached storage. This configuration is optimized for large in-memory databases, memory-intensive data processing, enterprise applications, and caching layers.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs across 16 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPUs but delivers a baseline network speed of 15 Gbps. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core, image, and text processing performance, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression and cryptography capabilities. The processor includes advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX), which accelerate workloads like small-scale machine learning inference and data compression. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per core makes it highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads. It is best suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It delivers top-tier single-core performance and is built for in-memory databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor running at 3.9 GHz.
tagline: A memory-intensive x86 platform featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and advanced Intel Xeon processing for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
512.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory providing 16.0 GB per core
Top-tier performance in single-core execution, image processing, and text processing benchmarks
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications
